summary: Retested BonoriyaAI floating-button click-to-open fix. ALL previously failing scenarios now PASS. Desktop real mouse click opens/closes the chat, mouse drag moves the button and does NOT open chat (position persisted in localStorage), /admin still excludes the widget on both desktop and mobile, mobile touch tap opens chat, and mobile touch drag moves the button without opening chat. Responsive widths (mobile=366, desktop=380) and resize clamping confirmed.
critical_code_review_comments: onPointerUp click-toggle uses the `dragging` state closure — since React state updates from setDragging in onPointerDown are asynchronous, synthetic pointerdown+pointerup dispatched in the same microtask see stale `dragging=false` and the toggle never fires. Real user pointer events work fine (verified by page.mouse.click). Any future automated test using dispatchEvent must insert an await between pointerdown and pointerup (>=50ms) to let React flush; alternatively the component could use a ref for dragging state to make it fully test-friendly.
vite.config.ts server.allowedHosts:true confirmed present — preview URL loads correctly.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The click-to-open regression is fully fixed by moving the toggle into onPointerUp (gated by !dragRef.current.moved) and wrapping setPointerCapture in try/catch. Note: test-only caveat — synthetic PointerEvents dispatched via evaluate() must have an async gap (>=50ms) between pointerdown and pointerup for the toggle to fire, because setDragging is a React state update, not a ref.
